etymology.description: 商(商) (shāng, "commerce") refers to business activities. 場(場) (chǎng, "field; place") denotes a specific location or space. Together, 商場(商場) (shāngchǎng, "shopping mall") signifies a place designated for commercial activities, where various shops and businesses are situated in a central location.
etymology.interpretation: 商(商) (shāng, "commerce") indicates business dealings, giving the sense that the location is centered around trade and consumer activities. 場(場) (chǎng, "field; place") implies a designated area, making 商場(商場) (shāngchǎng) a large, permanent site where commerce is concentrated, essentially capturing the concept of a shopping mall. The image of many different businesses coming together in the same field or site is the essence of this word.

examples.chinese: 这家商场(這家商場)非常大。
examples.english: This shopping mall is very large.

examples.chinese: 我们在商场(我們在商場)买到了很多东西(買到了很多東西)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài shāngchǎng mǎidào le hěn duō dōngxi.
examples.english: We bought many things at the shopping mall.

examples.chinese: 商场(商場)里有许多餐厅(裡有許多餐廳)。
examples.english: There are many restaurants in the shopping mall.
